items.text: Buried in the archives of the Philadelphia Naval Hospital, there is a series of death certificates from October nineteen forty-three. They are classified. The names are redacted. But the cause of death is visible. Molecular fusion.
items.gemini_reasoning: Introduces fictional medical records and a fictional cause of death ('Molecular fusion') as narrative elements.

items.text: Jacob was eighteen years old. He was a deck hand on the USS Eldridge. When the field generators were activated, he was standing on the aft deck, his hand resting on the railing. When the ship blinked out of phase, when it slipped into the gap, Jacob didn't go anywhere. He stayed right where he was. But the ship moved. Or the reality around the ship moved.
items.gemini_reasoning: Describes fictional events and details surrounding Jacob L. and the Philadelphia Experiment, including 'field generators' and 'blinking out of phase'.

items.text: The medics arrived. They tried to pull him away from the railing. They couldn't. The report states that the molecular cohesion between the subject's epidermis and the ferrous alloy of the railing is absolute.
items.gemini_reasoning: Describes fictional medical events and a fictional scientific explanation for Jacob's fusion with the railing.

items.text: It wasn't that his hand was stuck to the rail. His hand was the rail.
items.gemini_reasoning: A powerful, distinctive metaphor to describe the fusion.

items.text: Jacob's hand and the ship's railing became fused at an atomic level.

Jacob lived for three days. He was kept in a sedated twilight at the naval hospital. But the nurses reported something that defies biology. They said the steel railing, the chunk of metal fused to his wrist, was warm. Not body temperature warm. It was pulsing. It had a heartbeat. The circulation system of the boy had tried to integrate the metal. His blood was trying to pump through the steel. And for those three days, it succeeded. The phase shift hadn't just fused them. It had hybridized them.
items.gemini_reasoning: Describes a series of fictional biological anomalies and consequences of the 'phase shift' on Jacob and the railing.

items.text: It was pulsing. It had a heartbeat.
items.gemini_reasoning: Distinctive sensory description, personifying the steel railing.

Government agencies, specifically the FBI and Office of Alien Property, moved quickly to seize and classify Nikola Tesla's belongings after his death, acting with implied authority.
